summary: Neck dissection should be considered even with an undetermined primary tumor due to its role in achieving optimal oncologic outcomes through complete removal of involved lymph nodes. The decision hinges primarily on whether the disease is locally advanced but potentially curative, as confirmed via imaging and staging workup before proceeding surgically.
